title: Closing stdin of container from containers.Attach
body:
/kind feature

**Description**

Using the go bindings I stumbled across the issue that attaching to a container and passing a `Reader` as the `stdin` parameter (in my case an `io.PipeReader`) will keep the `stdin` of that container open, after the `Reader` returns `EOF`.

I think Docker does this by returning a special kind of struct called `HijackedResponse` which has a `CloseWrite` method. Calling this method actually closes the `stdin` of the container.

This feature is crucial for running shells or other repls and passing e.g. user input to them and (most importantly) getting the exit code of the shell.

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

comments.body:
Hi @repomaa, thanks for reaching out and apologies for the longer silence.

@baude @jwhonce @mheon what do you think? This might be something to consider when refactoring the bindings (not sure if that's still on the roadmap).

I think we need to figure out how the Docker API handles this and try and duplicate that behavior. I'm actually not sure how they handle closing one side of the HTTP connection, but they clearly have some way to do it.

@mheon This may impact a number of endpoints as we've been adding code to stop the endpoint when the client closes the connection. I'm not sure we've added any of that code to hijacked connections which is why I used "may".

@jwhonce We are not supporting the client *closing* the connection (which already works fine) - we just want to close one stream (STDIN). The remaining standard streams will continue streaming - we just stopped accepting input.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Negative. This is probably more of a @jwhonce and @baude thing than me since it will be entirely a bindings change.

@jwhonce @baude any progress on this?
Friendly ping.
I pretty sure I fixed some bugs like this, can you test again? I think if the reader returns EOF we close the stdin on the server side now.
I will close this, reopen if it is still broken...
I think the server works, but I don't know if the client does - original request was that the bindings we able to initiate a close of STDIN themselves.

Take this example `podman-remote run -i alpine cat <<<"test"`, this command would hang if we do not close the stdin correctly.
This works because the clients gets the EOF, then closes only the stdin stream to the server. The server now has a closed stdin stream thus closes the actual stdin for this container. Now `cat` gets EOF and exits successfully. If stdin is not closed this command would hang forever.
You could also just start a shell and press CTRL-D. The shell will exit correctly.
